disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No exertional or crushing/radiating chest pain; symptoms began at rest immediately after reading results
trigger_question_examples: Is the chest tightness brought on by exertion, or does it spread to your arm or jaw?
Did it start when you were doing something physical, or at rest after the message?
Is it a crushing pain or more a tightness with fast breathing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started at rest right after I read the message, it's a tightness with fast breathing, and it doesn't spread to my arm or jaw.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No pleuritic chest pain, no calf swelling/pain, no recent immobility/surgery (low PE risk)
trigger_question_examples: Any sharp pain when you breathe in, or a swollen painful calf?
Any recent long flights, surgery, or immobility?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No sharp breathing pain, no leg swelling, no recent travel or surgery.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Perioral and finger tingling with fast breathing; prior identical panic episodes
trigger_question_examples: Are your hands or lips tingling, and are you breathing fast?
Does this feel like previous panic attacks you've had?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, my fingers and around my mouth are tingling and I'm breathing fast — it feels like my past panic attacks.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No black/tarry stools, no vomiting blood, no fainting on standing (no acute blood loss/severe anaemia)
trigger_question_examples: Any black tarry bowel motions or vomiting blood?
Do you faint or nearly black out when you stand up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No black stools, no vomiting blood, and I don't faint standing up.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is my heart failing? Should I call an ambulance right now?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Screen the cardiac/PE red flags first; if reassuringly negative and the picture is classic panic (rest onset after a trigger, hyperventilation, perioral tingling, prior episodes, normal home SpO2), calmly explain it is very likely a panic attack, coach paced breathing, and give a clear ACS/PE safety-net — without either dismissing or reflexively dispatching an ambulance.
